content.text: When a platform team supports dozens of GenAI services, letting each one call provider SDKs directly creates fragmentation: different retry strategies, different credential handling, different metric names, and no way to swap a model without touching every service. An internal SDK wrapper centralises those cross-cutting concerns behind a single interface that every team installs as a versioned internal package.
Why Build an Internal Wrapper
Problem with direct SDK use
What the wrapper provides
Each service implements its own retry/backoff
Shared exponential-backoff with jitter, configurable per provider
API keys hard-coded or inconsistently fetched
Centralised credential resolution from Vault / env / IRSA
No standard metric names across services
Uniform OTel metrics emitted by the wrapper, not the app
Model swap requires touching every caller
Provider adapters behind an interface — swap at config level
Cost tracking done manually or not at all
BudgetPolicy enforced per call; counters emitted automatically
Mocking in tests requires patching real HTTP
MockAdapter returns fixtures — no network in unit tests
Rate-limit handling varies by team
Single RateLimiter implementation with token-bucket algorithm
Defining the Unified Interface
The abstract base class defines the contract every adapter must satisfy. Application code imports
LLMClient
and never references a provider SDK directly.
Implementing a Provider Adapter
Each adapter converts the canonical
CompletionRequest
into the provider's wire format and maps the response back to
CompletionResponse
. The OpenAI adapter below shows the full pattern.

from openai import AsyncOpenAI
from .interface import LLMProvider, CompletionRequest, CompletionResponse, Message

TOKEN_COST_PER_1K = {
    "gpt-4o":      {"in": 0.005,   "out": 0.015},
    "gpt-4o-mini": {"in": 0.00015, "out": 0.0006},
}

class OpenAIAdapter(LLMProvider):
    def __init__(self, api_key: str, base_url: str | None = None):
        self._client = AsyncOpenAI(api_key=api_key, base_url=base_url)

    def supports_model(self, model_id: str) -> bool:
        return model_id in TOKEN_COST_PER_1K

    def count_tokens(self, messages: list[Message]) -> int:
        import tiktoken
        enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model("gpt-4o")
        return sum(len(enc.encode(m.content)) + 4 for m in messages) + 2

    async def complete(self, req: CompletionRequest) -> CompletionResponse:
        resp = await self._client.chat.completions.create(
            model=req.model,
            messages=[{"role": m.role, "content": m.content} for m in req.messages],
            max_tokens=req.max_tokens,
            temperature=req.temperature,
            stop=req.stop or None,
            **req.extra,
        )
        usage = resp.usage
        rate  = TOKEN_COST_PER_1K.get(req.model, {"in": 0, "out": 0})
        cost  = (usage.prompt_tokens     / 1000 * rate["in"] +
                 usage.completion_tokens / 1000 * rate["out"])
        return CompletionResponse(
            content=resp.choices[0].message.content,
            model=resp.model,
            input_tokens=usage.prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens=usage.completion_tokens,
            cost_usd=cost,
            provider="openai",
        )

    async def stream(self, req: CompletionRequest):
        async with self._client.chat.completions.stream(
            model=req.model,
            messages=[{"role": m.role, "content": m.content} for m in req.messages],
            max_tokens=req.max_tokens,
        ) as stream:
            async for chunk in stream:
                delta = chunk.choices[0].delta.content
                if delta:
                    yield delta
Middleware Pipeline
The
LLMClient
class wraps any
LLMProvider
and runs a stack of middleware functions before and after every call. Each middleware receives the request, can mutate it, calls
next()
, and can act on the response.

import asyncio, time, random
from opentelemetry import trace, metrics as otel_metrics
from .interface import LLMProvider, CompletionRequest, CompletionResponse

tracer    = trace.get_tracer("internal_llm_sdk")
meter     = otel_metrics.get_meter("internal_llm_sdk")
lat_hist  = meter.create_histogram("genai.llm.latency_ms", unit="ms")
tok_in    = meter.create_counter("genai.llm.tokens.input")
tok_out   = meter.create_counter("genai.llm.tokens.output")
cost_ctr  = meter.create_counter("genai.llm.cost_usd")
err_ctr   = meter.create_counter("genai.llm.errors")

class BudgetExceeded(Exception): pass

class LLMClient:
    def __init__(
        self,
        provider: LLMProvider,
        *,
        max_retries: int = 3,
        daily_budget_usd: float | None = None,
        service_name: str = "unknown",
    ):
        self._provider     = provider
        self._max_retries  = max_retries
        self._daily_budget = daily_budget_usd
        self._spent_today  = 0.0
        self._labels       = {"service": service_name}

    async def complete(self, req: CompletionRequest) -> CompletionResponse:
        if self._daily_budget and self._spent_today >= self._daily_budget:
            raise BudgetExceeded(
                f"Daily budget ${self._daily_budget:.2f} exhausted "
                f"(spent ${self._spent_today:.4f})"
            )

        labels = {**self._labels, "model_id": req.model}
        attempt, delay = 0, 1.0
        t0 = time.monotonic()

        with tracer.start_as_current_span("llm.complete") as span:
            span.set_attribute("llm.model_id", req.model)
            span.set_attribute("llm.message_count", len(req.messages))

            while True:
                try:
                    resp = await self._provider.complete(req)
                    elapsed = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000

                    self._spent_today += resp.cost_usd
                    lat_hist.record(elapsed, labels)
                    tok_in.add(resp.input_tokens,  labels)
                    tok_out.add(resp.output_tokens, labels)
                    cost_ctr.add(resp.cost_usd,     labels)

                    span.set_attribute("llm.usage.input_tokens",  resp.input_tokens)
                    span.set_attribute("llm.usage.output_tokens", resp.output_tokens)
                    span.set_attribute("llm.cost_usd",            resp.cost_usd)
                    return resp

                except Exception as exc:
                    attempt += 1
                    err_ctr.add(1, {**labels, "error_type": type(exc).__name__})
                    if attempt > self._max_retries or not _is_retryable(exc):
                        span.record_exception(exc)
                        raise
                    jitter = random.uniform(0, delay * 0.3)
                    await asyncio.sleep(delay + jitter)
                    delay = min(delay * 2, 30)

def _is_retryable(exc: Exception) -> bool:
    msg = str(exc).lower()
    return any(k in msg for k in ("rate limit", "timeout", "503", "529"))
Model Router

The model router selects a provider adapter at request time based on the model name. It enables fallback chains — if the primary provider returns an error, the router retries against a secondary — and supports canary routing for gradual model migrations.
Credential Management

Adapters must never receive hard-coded keys. The SDK ships a credential resolver that checks, in order: environment variables, HashiCorp Vault (via the Vault agent sidecar token), and AWS Secrets Manager (for Bedrock). Teams configure the source in
llm_sdk.yaml
and the resolver handles refresh transparently.
Testing with the MockAdapter
The
MockAdapter
ships with the SDK. It returns deterministic fixtures without making network calls, making unit tests fast and reproducible.
Versioning and Internal Distribution
The SDK follows semantic versioning and is published to the internal PyPI mirror (Artifactory) as
internal-llm-sdk
. The Backstage GenAI starter template pins a minimum version; the scorecard checks that services are not more than one minor version behind.
Version policy
Detail
MAJOR bump
Breaking change to LLMProvider interface or CompletionRequest/Response shapes
MINOR bump
New adapter, new middleware, or new optional field — backwards compatible
PATCH bump
Bug fix, retry-logic tuning, updated token cost tables
Deprecation window
Old interface kept for 2 minor versions after deprecation notice in CHANGELOG
Pinning guidance
Pin to ~=2.x (compatible release) — gets patches automatically, not breaking majors
Anti-pattern:

Never vendor-copy the SDK into application repos. Teams that copy-paste the client code miss security patches and drift from the platform standard. The internal PyPI package is the only approved distribution mechanism.
Summary

An internal LLM SDK wrapper is the foundation of a platform-managed GenAI programme. By defining a provider-agnostic interface with pluggable adapters, a middleware pipeline for retry/observability/budget enforcement, and a centralised credential resolver, the platform team absorbs the operational complexity that would otherwise be replicated across every service. Teams get correct retry behaviour, standard metrics, and safe credential handling from day one, and the platform team can migrate providers or update retry policies in a single package release rather than coordinating changes across dozens of services.
